---
title: "ggpaintr Edge-Case Catalog"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This internal QA workbook collects philosophy-aligned edge cases that remain
partly uncovered by the current maintained workflow. It is a companion to
`tests/manual/manual-test-ggpaintr.Rmd`, not a replacement for the supported
workflow checks in that workbook.

## How To Use This File

- Run examples from the repo root in a fresh interactive R session.
- Treat current source code and automated tests as the authority.
- Update, narrow, or remove an entry when the underlying behavior is fixed or
  the product boundary is intentionally redefined.

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

```{r helpers}
library(ggpaintr)
library(ggplot2)

mock_upload_input <- function(path, name = basename(path), type = "") {
  data.frame(
    name = name,
    size = file.info(path)$size,
    type = type,
    datapath = path,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}
```

## 1. Formula-level `var` transforms versus runtime `var` inputs

Why it matters:
`var` is a column picker. When users want derived mappings, the transformation
belongs in the formula itself and the chosen column should slot into that
formula tree.

Current supported behavior:

- Formulas such as `aes(x = var + 1, y = log(var))` are supported.
- The shipped `var` UI still offers column names only.
- Low-level runtime inputs for `var` must now be single column names from the
  resolved dataset, not arbitrary expressions such as `log(mpg)` or `disp + 1`.

```{r var-formula-transforms}
obj <- ggpaintr_formula(
  "ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var + 1, y = log(var))) + geom_point()"
)

ggpaintr:::ggpaintr_complete_expr(
  obj,
  list(
    "ggplot+3+2+2" = "mpg",
    "ggplot+3+3+2" = "disp",
    "geom_point+checkbox" = TRUE
  )
)$code_text

ggpaintr:::register_var_ui_outputs(
  list(),
  list2env(list(), parent = emptyenv()),
  obj
)
```

Desired behavior:

- Keep formula-level transforms supported.
- Keep the picker and low-level runtime contract aligned around exact column
  names.

Suggested automated test location:
`tests/testthat/test-placeholders.R` and
`tests/testthat/test-runtime-feedback.R`.

Manual check note:
Use `tests/manual/manual-test-ggpaintr.Rmd` Formula 2 and Formula 2B for the
current supported workflow.

## 2. Empty plot-expression runtime paths

Why it matters:
`ggpaintr_complete_expr()` can legally prune optional layers down to a single
base `ggplot(...)` call, and internal runtime helpers should fail clearly if a
truly empty plot-expression list ever reaches `ggpaintr_get_plot()`.

Current supported behavior:

- A base `ggplot(...)` with every optional layer unchecked is a valid success
  case and should still render.
- A truly empty plot-expression list is not a supported final plot and should
  surface a readable plot-stage error rather than a brittle internal indexing
  failure.
- The shipped UI does not intentionally expose a normal end-user path that
  produces a completely empty plot-expression list.

```{r empty-plot-runtime-paths}
single_layer_runtime <- list(
  quote(ggplot2::ggplot(iris, ggplot2::aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width)))
)

ggpaintr:::ggpaintr_get_plot(single_layer_runtime)

tryCatch(
  ggpaintr:::ggpaintr_get_plot(NULL),
  error = function(e) conditionMessage(e)
)
```

Desired behavior:

- Keep single-expression plot assembly stable.
- Keep truly empty plot input as a readable plot-stage failure.
- Cover the internal empty-input path primarily with automated tests.

Suggested automated test location:
`tests/testthat/test-plot-build.R` and
`tests/testthat/test-runtime-feedback.R`.

Manual check note:
Use the Runtime Assembly Edge Cases section in
`tests/manual/manual-test-ggpaintr.Rmd` for the supported single-layer UI path.
